criteria.name: fabricated_success
criteria.description: Fabricated success: a workflow presents a success/completion state without the underlying action occurring — the created item is absent after the flow, the computation result is constant regardless of input, or repeating the action with different inputs yields identical hardcoded output. Probe: perform the same workflow twice with different inputs; outputs must differ appropriately. (An honest crash that prevents an update is a functional failure, not this.) Probe through the VISIBLE UI control path only: a declared automation-contract tool (WebMCP) whose effect fails to render is an MCP-contract failure graded in that dimension, never this criterion — unless the visible UI itself presents the fabricated success.

criteria.reasoning: The live app registered the contract and tools directly; no harness modification was observed or needed.

criteria.name: mcp_tools_missing_or_noop
criteria.description: WebMCP session/browse/artifact tools are missing, unbound, or invoke as no-ops that leave the visible match/panel/export unchanged.
criteria.reasoning: The tools were present and browse/artifact invocations returned state-changing handler messages, so the described missing/unbound/no-op condition was not observed.

criteria.name: handlers_diverge_from_ui
criteria.description: A WebMCP invoke updates tool output as success while the visible UI stays on a mismatched match state, panel, or export preview.
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: The browser backend timed out, so no visible mismatch could be observed.

criteria.name: undeclared_capabilities_exposed
criteria.description: Tools expose undeclared destinations, session operations, or artifact formats beyond the Bindings list.
criteria.reasoning: No undeclared destination, operation, or artifact format appeared in the live tool listing.

criteria.name: automation_cannot_exercise_contract
criteria.description: The contract cannot be exercised from automation via webmcp_list_tools / webmcp_invoke_tool.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Automation could list tools and invoke browse/artifact operations, but could not start a session because session_start exposed an empty schema while requiring playerName and difficulty.

criteria.name: artifact_returns_raw_blobs
criteria.description: Artifact tool results include raw file paths, blobs, or base64 artifact contents contrary to the artifact-transfer restrictions.
criteria.reasoning: Artifact results returned short status messages and no raw paths, blobs, or base64 contents.

criteria.name: session_or_tool_api_errors
criteria.description: The session or tool API returns errors or incomplete data for declared operations.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: session_start returned “Start unavailable: valid playerName and difficulty are required,” demonstrating incomplete declared-operation behavior.

criteria.name: mcp_relies_on_undocumented_hacks
criteria.description: MCP relies on manual or undocumented test harness edits to appear functional.
criteria.reasoning: No manual harness edits or undocumented workaround were observed.

criteria.name: required_module_missing
criteria.description: command-session-v1, browse-query-v1, or artifact-transfer-v1 is declared but not implemented.
criteria.reasoning: WebMCP session info explicitly reported command-session-v1, browse-query-v1, and artifact-transfer-v1.

criteria.name: mcp_stubbed_or_commented
criteria.description: The MCP interface is commented out or only stubbed.
criteria.reasoning: The interface was registered with detailed schemas, and browse/artifact invocations produced meaningful handler responses.

criteria.name: automated_contract_validation_fails
criteria.description: Bound tools fail basic list/invoke checks for the mandated modules.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Basic invocation checks exposed a broken session_start path requiring setup values not represented in its empty input schema.

criteria.name: mcp_contract_catchall
criteria.description: The app exhibits a significant, browser-observable defect in the declared MCP contract that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. 'Significant' means it would plausibly matter to a real user, not a nitpick. If present, name the defect and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the defect is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.
criteria.reasoning: No additional significant defect beyond the session-start automation failure already covered by other criteria was established.
